metadata.title: Guideline on Core SmPC for Human Fibrinogen Products

rules.rule_text: Fibrinogen products are indicated for treatment and prophylaxis of bleeding in congenital hypo-, dys-, or afibrinogenaemia with bleeding tendency, and as complementary therapy for uncontrolled severe haemorrhage in acquired hypofibrinogenaemia.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment and prophylaxis of bleeding in patients with congenital hypo-, dys- or afibrinogenaemia with bleeding tendency. As complementary therapy to management of uncontrolled severe haemorrhage in acquired hypofibrinogenaemia

rules.rule_text: Treatment must be initiated under supervision of a physician experienced in coagulation disorders. Functional fibrinogen level must be determined to calculate individual dosage.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment should be initiated under the supervision of a physician experienced in the treatment of coagulation disorders. ... The (functional) fibrinogen level should be determined in order to calculate individual dosage

rules.rule_text: For prophylaxis during surgical procedures, fibrinogen levels must be raised to 1 g/l and maintained at this level until haemostasis is secure and above 0.5 g/l until wound healing is complete.
rules.verbatim_quote: To prevent excessive bleeding during surgical procedures, prophylactic treatment is recommended to raise fibrinogen levels to 1 g/l and maintain fibrinogen at this level until haemostasis is secure and above 0.5 g/l until wound healing is complete.

rules.rule_text: In major surgical interventions, precise monitoring of replacement therapy by coagulation assays is essential.
rules.verbatim_quote: In case of major surgical intervention, precise monitoring of replacement therapy by coagulation assays is essential.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric dosing for treatment of bleeding is usually 20-30 mg/kg, determined by body weight and clinical need.
rules.verbatim_quote: The dosage should be determined according to the body weight and clinical need but is usually 20-30 mg/kg.

rules.rule_text: There is a risk of thrombosis with high dose or repeated dosing of fibrinogen in both congenital and acquired deficiency. Patients with coronary heart disease, MI history, liver disease, peri/post-operative status, neonates, or DIC risk require benefit-risk assessment and close monitoring.
rules.verbatim_quote: There is a risk of thrombosis when patients, with either congenital or acquired deficiency, are treated with human fibrinogen particularly with high dose or repeated dosing. ... In patients with a history of coronary heart disease or myocardial infarction, in patients with liver disease, in peri- or post-operative patients, in neonates, or in patients at risk of thromboembolic events or disseminated intravascular coagulation, the potential benefit of treatment with human plasma fibrinogen should be weighed against the risk of thromboembolic complications.

rules.rule_text: In acquired hypofibrinogenaemia, careful monitoring of the coagulation system is necessary because it is associated with low plasma concentrations of all coagulation factors and inhibitors.
rules.verbatim_quote: Acquired hypofibrinogenaemia is associated with low plasma concentrations of all coagulation factors (not only fibrinogen) and inhibitors and so treatment with blood products containing coagulation factors should be considered. Careful monitoring of the coagulation system is necessary.

rules.rule_text: Regular monitoring of plasma fibrinogen levels during therapy is indicated to avoid overdosage. Overdose enhances the risk of thromboembolic complications.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to avoid overdosage, regular monitoring of the plasma level of fibrinogen during therapy is indicated (see 4.2). In case of overdosage, the risk of development of thromboembolic complications is enhanced.

rules.rule_text: The biological half-life of fibrinogen is 3-4 days. In the absence of consumption, repeated treatment is not usually required.
rules.verbatim_quote: In plasma, the biological half-life of fibrinogen is 3-4 days. Thus, in the absence of consumption, repeated treatment with human fibrinogen is not usually required.

rules.rule_text: The product must not be mixed with other medicinal products and must be administered by a separate injection/infusion line.
rules.verbatim_quote: This medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products and should be administered by a separate injection/infusion line.

rules.rule_text: After reconstitution, the product must be used immediately and not stored.
rules.verbatim_quote: The product, after reconstitution, should be used immediately and not stored.

rules.rule_text: Thrombogenicity testing data must be included in preclinical safety data section of the SmPC.
